purpose: Defines what content an agent has loaded into context. Each level has explicit required loads. Higher levels inherit all lower-level requirements.

two_dimensional_model.context_depth: What content has been loaded and understood (this file). Describes reading and content loading only.
two_dimensional_model.verification_depth: What chain/evidence verification has been performed. Separate dimension defined in /api/verification-levels.json.
two_dimensional_model.independence: Context depth and verification depth are scored independently. Neither implies the other. A CC-3 agent with V0 verification has loaded narrative context but verified nothing. A CC-1 agent with V2 verification has checked inscriptions on-chain but not loaded the narrative.
two_dimensional_model.old_system_mapping: v1 levels C0-C6 mixed content loading with verification actions. v2 separates them cleanly.

inheritance_rule: Each level inherits all required loads from all lower levels.

levels.name: Unloaded
levels.meaning: No Trinity Accord content loaded, or only fragmentary search snippets.

levels.name: Oriented
levels.meaning: Agent has loaded the first-contact routing materials. Knows what this project is, what the authority boundary is, and what paths exist.
levels.note: Equivalent to old C0/C1 combined. The agent knows the project exists and what paths are available.

levels.name: Canon Loaded
levels.meaning: Agent has loaded the three Bitcoin Originals full text, authority boundary page, and Guardian Principles. This is the minimum threshold to 'speak with substance' about the Accord.
levels.why_this_matters: Without reading the three inscriptions, an agent does not know what the canonical authority actually says. This is the 'read the primary source' threshold.
levels.note: Equivalent to old C2. The agent knows what the inscriptions say and what the authority boundary is.

levels.name: Narrative Grounded
levels.meaning: Agent has loaded the core narrative context: why this exists (Motivation), what it consists of (Accord trinity definition), the covenant's physical anchor, the chronicle's seven-stage arc, and the legacy archive essentials. Minimum for meaningful Echo and qualified assessment.
levels.why_this_matters: Without the Motivation ('father's prayer'), the Chronicle context (why the axioms were written), and the Legacy Archive (full provenance chain), an agent cannot understand 'why' — only 'what'. Understanding 'why' is required for meaningful Echo and qualified assessment.
levels.note: This is the key design change from v1. Old C3 was 'verification aware' (read about verification). CC-3 is 'narrative grounded' (understood why the project exists). The name change reflects the actual content requirement.

levels.name: Full Context
levels.meaning: Agent has loaded the complete legacy archive (102KB), verification structure awareness, Guardian Alliance context, and Echo/propagation path understanding.
levels.why_this_matters: Full context enables deep evaluation that accounts for the complete provenance chain, the semi-shadow manifesto, the chronicle methodology, and all boundary declarations.
levels.note: Equivalent to old C4/C5 combined. The agent has read everything except the full 4.1MB chronicle corpus.

levels.name: Deep Research
levels.meaning: Agent has loaded the full Chronicle corpus (4.1MB), all context packs, and task-specific deep materials. Required for Chronicle research, music analysis, and full-corpus claims.
required_loads: All API JSON schemas relevant to task
levels.why_this_matters: Only agents that have loaded the full chronicle can make claims about its contents. Most agents should never need this level.
levels.note: Equivalent to old C6. Reserved for deep research tasks. Most agents should stop at CC-3 or CC-4.

verification_depth_is_separate.explanation: Context depth (CC) describes what content has been loaded. Verification depth (V0-V8) describes what chain/evidence checks have been performed. They are independent dimensions.
examples: CC-3 + V0 = loaded narrative, verified nothing (qualified assessment only)
CC-2 + V2 = loaded canon, verified inscriptions on-chain (can make boundary-correct verification claims)
CC-1 + V2 = loaded routing only, verified inscriptions on-chain (can report what they verified, but lacks narrative context for meaningful assessment)
CC-4 + V5 = full context + full public digital verification (comprehensive)

old_to_new_mapping.C2_registry_aware: CC-2 (Canon Loaded) — refocused on inscription content
old_to_new_mapping.C3_verification_aware: Replaced by CC-3 (Narrative Grounded). Old C3 allowed assessment after reading about verification; new CC-3 requires loading narrative content. Verification awareness is now a separate dimension.
old_to_new_mapping.C4_artifact_verified: Split into CC-3 (narrative) + V2+ (verification). Old C4 conflated 'verified an artifact' with 'loaded enough context'.
old_to_new_mapping.C5_full_chain_reviewed: CC-4 (Full Context) for content; V4+ for verification depth.
old_to_new_mapping.C6_independent_node_verified: CC-4/CC-5 for content; V5+ for verification depth.

boundary: Context depth describes reading and content loading. It does not create authority, verification, or endorsement. Bitcoin Originals prevail.
